revision.comment: Bot: append Wikidata-derived interwikis
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = [[File:{{ill|Takarazuka Yasaka Shrine Entrance|ja|Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja1|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takarazuka Yasaka Shrine Entrance|qq=}}.jpg|300px]] | image description = Torii at Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Takarazuka, Hyōgo|Takarazuka City]], Hirai 2-16-14 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|32|N|135|23|22|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}{{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|qq=}} | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}} | founded = (unknown) | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | alternate_names = Yasaka Shrine | festival = Approximately on Saturday and Sunday around October 27 | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=49|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=135|lon_min=23|lon_sec=22|label=Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |image = Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja1.jpg }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=}}) in Takarazuka City is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Hirai, Takarazuka, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It originally belonged to the village shrine system of the former Kawabe District in the village of Hirai.
Its annual festival is celebrated with a Danjiri Festival featuring a type of ornate festival float characteristic of Takarazuka.
== Deity == The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|qq=}}.
== History == * In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited. * Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]. * In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine. * In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.
== Shrines and Grounds == [[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]] * Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684).
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)
== Cultural Properties == * The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.
== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the [[Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line]] station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|qq=}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|qq=}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and a treasure pagoda ([[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]]) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Yasaka Shrines
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = [[File:{{ill|Takarazuka Yasaka Shrine Entrance|ja|Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja1|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takarazuka Yasaka Shrine Entrance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.jpg|300px]] | image description = Torii at Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Takarazuka, Hyōgo|Takarazuka City]], Hirai 2-16-14 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|32|N|135|23|22|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}{{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = (unknown) | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | alternate_names = Yasaka Shrine | festival = Approximately on Saturday and Sunday around October 27 | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=49|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=135|lon_min=23|lon_sec=22|label=Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |image = Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja1.jpg }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}) in Takarazuka City is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Hirai, Takarazuka, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It originally belonged to the village shrine system of the former Kawabe District in the village of Hirai.
Its annual festival is celebrated with a Danjiri Festival featuring a type of ornate festival float characteristic of Takarazuka.
== Deity == The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== History == * In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited. * Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]. * In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine. * In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.
== Shrines and Grounds == [[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]] * Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684).
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)
== Cultural Properties == * The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.
== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the [[Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line]] station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|qq=}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|qq=}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and a treasure pagoda ([[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]]) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]

== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line]] station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|qq=}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|qq=}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and a treasure pagoda ([[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]]) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11391825]] Wikidata name: Yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = [[File:{{ill|Takarazuka Yasaka Shrine Entrance|ja|Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja1|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takarazuka Yasaka Shrine Entrance|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.jpg|300px]] | image description = Torii at Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Takarazuka, Hyōgo|Takarazuka City]], Hirai 2-16-14 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|49|32|N|135|23|22|E|scale:5000_region:JP-28_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}{{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = (unknown) | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | alternate_names = Yasaka Shrine | festival = Approximately on Saturday and Sunday around October 27 | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=49|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=135|lon_min=23|lon_sec=22|label=Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |image = Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja1.jpg }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}) in Takarazuka City is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Hirai, Takarazuka, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It originally belonged to the village shrine system of the former Kawabe District in the village of Hirai.
Its annual festival is celebrated with a Danjiri Festival featuring a type of ornate festival float characteristic of Takarazuka.
== Deity == The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== History == * In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited. * Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]. * In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine. * In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.
== Shrines and Grounds == [[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]] * Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684).
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)
== Cultural Properties == * The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.
== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line]] station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|qq=}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|qq=}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and a treasure pagoda ([[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]]) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]

== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line]] station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|qq=|1=Yamamoto Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|qq=|1=Daihō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and a treasure pagoda ([[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]]) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|村社|" to "|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11391825]] Wikidata name: Yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja1.jpg | image description = Torii at Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Takarazuka, Hyōgo|Takarazuka City]], Hirai 2-16-14 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.825556|N|135.389444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993}} | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = (unknown) | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | alternate_names = Yasaka Shrine | festival = Approximately on Saturday and Sunday around October 27 | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=49|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=135|lon_min=23|lon_sec=22|label=Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Takarazuka|qid=Q823967}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}) in Takarazuka City is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Hirai, Takarazuka, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It originally belonged to the village shrine system of the former Kawabe District in the village of Hirai.
Its annual festival is celebrated with a Danjiri Festival featuring a type of ornate festival float characteristic of Takarazuka.
== Deity == The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== History == * In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited. * Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]. * In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine. * In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.
== Shrines and Grounds == [[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]] * Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684).
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)
== Cultural Properties == * The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.
== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line]] station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|qq=|1=Yamamoto Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|qq=|1=Daihō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and a treasure pagoda ([[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]]) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]

== History == * In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited. * Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]. * In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine. * In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.
== Shrines and Grounds == [[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]] * Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684).
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)
== Cultural Properties == * The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.
== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line]] station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|qq=|1=Yamamoto Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|qq=|1=Daihō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and a treasure pagoda ([[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]]) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Takarazuka)]][[ja:八阪神社 (宝塚市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11391825]] Wikidata name: Yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja1.jpg | image description = Torii at Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Takarazuka, Hyōgo|Takarazuka City]], Hirai 2-16-14 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.825556|N|135.389444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|qid=Q272993}} | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = (unknown) | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | alternate_names = Yasaka Shrine | festival = Approximately on Saturday and Sunday around October 27 | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=49|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=135|lon_min=23|lon_sec=22|label=Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Takarazuka|qid=Q823967}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}) in Takarazuka City is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Hirai, Takarazuka, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It originally belonged to the village shrine system of the former Kawabe District in the village of Hirai.
Its annual festival is celebrated with a Danjiri Festival featuring a type of ornate festival float characteristic of Takarazuka.
== Deity == The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== History == * In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited. * Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]. * In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine. * In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.
== Shrines and Grounds == [[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]] * Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684).
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)
== Cultural Properties == * The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.
== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line]] station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|qq=|1=Yamamoto Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|qq=|1=Daihō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and a treasure pagoda ([[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]]) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Takarazuka)]][[ja:八阪神社 (宝塚市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11391825]] Wikidata name: Yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja1.jpg | image description = Torii at Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Takarazuka, Hyōgo|Takarazuka City]], Hirai 2-16-14 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.825556|N|135.389444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = (unknown) | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | alternate_names = Yasaka Shrine | festival = Approximately on Saturday and Sunday around October 27 | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=49|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=135|lon_min=23|lon_sec=22|label=Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Takarazuka|WD=Q823967}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}) in Takarazuka City is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Hirai, Takarazuka, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It originally belonged to the village shrine system of the former Kawabe District in the village of Hirai.
Its annual festival is celebrated with a Danjiri Festival featuring a type of ornate festival float characteristic of Takarazuka.
== Deity == The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== History == * In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited. * Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]. * In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine. * In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.
== Shrines and Grounds == [[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]] * Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684).
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)
== Cultural Properties == * The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.
== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line]] station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|1=Yamamoto Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|1=Daihō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and a treasure pagoda ([[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]]) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Takarazuka)]][[ja:八阪神社 (宝塚市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11391825]] Wikidata name: Yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja1.jpg | image description = Torii at Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Takarazuka, Hyōgo|Takarazuka City]], Hirai 2-16-14 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.825556|N|135.389444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = (unknown) | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | alternate_names = Yasaka Shrine | festival = Approximately on Saturday and Sunday around October 27 | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=49|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=135|lon_min=23|lon_sec=22|label=Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Takarazuka|WD=Q823967}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}) in Takarazuka City is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Hirai, Takarazuka, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It originally belonged to the village shrine system of the former Kawabe District in the village of Hirai.
Its annual festival is celebrated with a Danjiri Festival featuring a type of ornate festival float characteristic of Takarazuka.
== Deity == The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== History == * In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited. * Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]. * In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine. * In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.
== Shrines and Grounds == [[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]] * Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684).
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)
== Cultural Properties == * The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.
== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line]] station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|1=Yamamoto Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|1=Daihō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and a treasure pagoda ([[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]]) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Takarazuka)]][[ja:八阪神社 (宝塚市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11391825]] Wikidata name: Yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja1.jpg | image description = Torii at Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Takarazuka, Hyōgo|Takarazuka City]], Hirai 2-16-14 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.825556|N|135.389444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = (unknown) | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | alternate_names = Yasaka Shrine | festival = Approximately on Saturday and Sunday around October 27 | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=49|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=135|lon_min=23|lon_sec=22|label=Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Takarazuka|WD=Q823967}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}) in Takarazuka City is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Hirai, Takarazuka, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It originally belonged to the village shrine system of the former Kawabe District in the village of Hirai.
Its annual festival is celebrated with a Danjiri Festival featuring a type of ornate festival float characteristic of Takarazuka.
== Deity == The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== History == * In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited. * Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]. * In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine. * In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.
== Shrines and Grounds == [[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]] * Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684).
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)
== Cultural Properties == * The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.
== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line]] station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|1=Yamamoto Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|1=Daihō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and a treasure pagoda ([[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]]) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]

== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line]] station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|1=Yamamoto Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|1=Daihō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and a treasure pagoda ([[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]]) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yasaka-jinja (Takarazuka)]][[ja:八阪神社 (宝塚市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11391825]] Wikidata name: Yasaka-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]] | image = File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja1.jpg | image description = Torii at Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | location = [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[:en:Takarazuka, Hyōgo|Takarazuka City]], Hirai 2-16-14 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.825556|N|135.389444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = (unknown) | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | alternate_names = Yasaka Shrine | festival = Approximately on Saturday and Sunday around October 27 | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=49|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=135|lon_min=23|lon_sec=22|label=Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Takarazuka|WD=Q823967}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}) in Takarazuka City is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Hirai, Takarazuka, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It originally belonged to the village shrine system of the former Kawabe District in the village of Hirai.
Its annual festival is celebrated with a Danjiri Festival featuring a type of ornate festival float characteristic of Takarazuka.
== Deity == The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== History == * In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited. * Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]. * In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine. * In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.
== Shrines and Grounds == [[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]] * Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684).
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)
== Cultural Properties == * The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.
== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line]] station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|1=Yamamoto Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|1=Daihō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and a treasure pagoda ([[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]]) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==

== History == * In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited. * Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]. * In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine. * In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.
== Shrines and Grounds == [[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]] * Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684).
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)
== Cultural Properties == * The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.
== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line]] station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|1=Yamamoto Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|1=Daihō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and a treasure pagoda ([[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]]) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]

founded = (unknown) | main_hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Ryūzō]] | alternate_names = Yasaka Shrine | festival = Approximately on Saturday and Sunday around October 27 | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=49|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=135|lon_min=23|lon_sec=22|label=Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Takarazuka|WD=Q823967}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}) in Takarazuka City is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto Shrine]] located in Hirai, Takarazuka, [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]. It originally belonged to the village shrine system of the former Kawabe District in the village of Hirai.
Its annual festival is celebrated with a Danjiri Festival featuring a type of ornate festival float characteristic of Takarazuka.
== Deity == The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== History == * In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited. * Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tennō]]. * In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine. * In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.
== Shrines and Grounds == [[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]] * Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of [[:en:Jōkyō|Jōkyō]] (1684).
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)
== Cultural Properties == * The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.
== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the [[:en:Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line]] station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|1=Yamamoto Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|1=Daihō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] and a treasure pagoda ([[:en:Hōkyōintō|Hōkyōintō]]) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * [[:en:Yasaka Shrine|Yasaka Shrine]] * [[:en:Gion cult|Gion Faith]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}} | image = File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja1.jpg | image description = Torii at Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|Takarazuka, Hyōgo|en|Takarazuka, Hyōgo|WD=Q823967|lt=Takarazuka City}}, Hirai 2-16-14 | coordinates = {{Coord|34.825556|N|135.389444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Susanoo|WD=Q272993}} | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = (unknown) | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Ryūzō}} | alternate_names = Yasaka Shrine | festival = Approximately on Saturday and Sunday around October 27 | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=49|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=135|lon_min=23|lon_sec=22|label=Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City)|position=center|width=200|float=center}} | location = {{ill|Takarazuka|WD=Q823967}} | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}) in Takarazuka City is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in Hirai, Takarazuka, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It originally belonged to the village shrine system of the former Kawabe District in the village of Hirai.
Its annual festival is celebrated with a Danjiri Festival featuring a type of ornate festival float characteristic of Takarazuka.
== Deity == The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== History == * In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited. * Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}. * In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine. * In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.
== Shrines and Grounds == [[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]] * Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} (1684).
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)
== Cultural Properties == * The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.
== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the {{ill|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|en|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|WD=Q3832859|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|1=Yamamoto Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|1=Daihō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}} and a treasure pagoda ({{ill|Hōkyōintō|en|Hōkyōintō|WD=Q3146365|lt=Hōkyōintō}}) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}}

'''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}) in Takarazuka City is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine}} located in Hirai, Takarazuka, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It originally belonged to the village shrine system of the former Kawabe District in the village of Hirai. 

Its annual festival is celebrated with a Danjiri Festival featuring a type of ornate festival float characteristic of Takarazuka.

== Deity ==
The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== History ==
* In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited.
* Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}.
* In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine.
* In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.

== Shrines and Grounds ==
[[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]]
* Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} (1684).

== Festivals and Annual Events ==
* The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)

== Cultural Properties ==
* The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.

== Access ==
* A 8‑minute walk from the {{ill|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|en|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|WD=Q3832859|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|1=Yamamoto Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.

== Surroundings ==
* Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|1=Daihō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}} and a treasure pagoda ({{ill|Hōkyōintō|en|Hōkyōintō|WD=Q3146365|lt=Hōkyōintō}}) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.

== References ==

{{Reflist}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}}
* {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}}

The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

* Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}.

It originally belonged to the village shrine system of the former Kawabe District in the village of Hirai.
Its annual festival is celebrated with a Danjiri Festival featuring a type of ornate festival float characteristic of Takarazuka.
== Deity == The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== History == * In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited. * Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō}}. * In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine. * In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.
== Shrines and Grounds == [[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]] * Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō}} (1684).
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)
== Cultural Properties == * The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.
== Access == * A 8‑minute walk from the {{ill|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|en|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|WD=Q3832859|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|1=Yamamoto Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|1=Daihō-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}} and a treasure pagoda ({{ill|Hōkyōintō|en|Hōkyōintō|WD=Q3146365|lt=Hōkyōintō}}) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.
== References ==
{{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|en|Yasaka Shrine|WD=Q692714|lt=Yasaka Shrine}} * {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith}}

== Shrines and Grounds == [[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]] * Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō}} (1684).
== Festivals and Annual Events == * The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)
== Cultural Properties == * The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.
== Access == * A 8-minute walk from the {{ill|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|en|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|WD=Q3832859|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|1=Q23806826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.
== Surroundings == * Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|1=Q11434344|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} and a treasure pagoda ({{ill|Hōkyōintō|en|Hōkyōintō|WD=Q3146365|lt=Hōkyōintō|1=Hōkyōintō}}) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.

'''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}) in Takarazuka City is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hirai, Takarazuka, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It originally belonged to the village shrine system of the former Kawabe District in the village of Hirai. 

Its annual festival is celebrated with a Danjiri Festival featuring a type of ornate festival float characteristic of Takarazuka.

== Deity ==
The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== History ==
* In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited.
* Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}.
* In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine.
* In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.

== Shrines and Grounds ==
[[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]]
* Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō}} (1684).

== Festivals and Annual Events ==
* The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)

== Cultural Properties ==
* The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.

== Access ==
* A 8-minute walk from the {{ill|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|en|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|WD=Q3832859|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|1=Q23806826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.

== Surroundings ==
* Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|1=Q11434344|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} and a treasure pagoda ({{ill|Hōkyōintō|en|Hōkyōintō|WD=Q3146365|lt=Hōkyōintō|1=Hōkyōintō}}) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.

{{infobox religious building
 | name = Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City)
 | native_name = {{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}

 | image description = Torii at Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City)
 | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}} {{ill|Takarazuka, Hyōgo|en|Takarazuka, Hyōgo|WD=Q823967|lt=Takarazuka City|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}, Hirai 2-16-14

 | type = {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}

 | main_hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Ryūzō|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
 | alternate_names = Yasaka Shrine
 | festival = Approximately on Saturday and Sunday around October 27
 | map = {{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=49|lat_sec=32|lon_deg=135|lon_min=23|lon_sec=22|label=Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City)|position=center|width=200|float=center}}
| location = {{ill|Takarazuka|WD=Q823967|1=Takarazuka, Hyōgo}}
 | architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}
 }}
'''Yasaka Shrine''' ({{ill|Yasaka Shrine|ja|八阪神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}) in Takarazuka City is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto Shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Hirai, Takarazuka, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It originally belonged to the village shrine system of the former Kawabe District in the village of Hirai. 

Its annual festival is celebrated with a Danjiri Festival featuring a type of ornate festival float characteristic of Takarazuka.

== Deity ==
The main deity is {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} (Susanoo-no-Mikoto), with the secondary (or co-enshrined) deity being {{ill|Hakusan-hime|ja|白山姫命|1=Kukurihime|lt=Shirayama Hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan-hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Kukurihime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

== History ==
* In the early 10th century, the local tradition states that the shrine was enshrined by the children of Utsunomiya Yazasaburō Nakatsugi and Shigetuni after being invited.
* Until the Edo period, it was known as the Ushinotengū (【ill|Gyūtōtennō Shrine|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō社】) dedicated to {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozu Tennō|1=Gozu Tennō}}.
* In the second year of the Meiji period (1869), following the 【ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令】, the enshrined deity’s name was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} and the shrine’s name was changed to Yasaka Shrine.
* In Showa 41 (1966), the shrine absorbed the Hakusan Shrine (formerly known as the Sannō Gongen Shrine) and has remained so since.

== Shrines and Grounds ==
[[File:Takarazuka-Yasaka-jinja2.jpg|thumb|Yasaka Shrine (Takarazuka City) – Haiden]]
* Main hall: A one-bay structure in the Ryūzō style built in the mid-16th century and repaired in the first year of {{ill|Jōkyō|en|Jōkyō|WD=Q1146221|lt=Jōkyō|1=Jōkyō}} (1684).

== Festivals and Annual Events ==
* The main festival is held around October 27 (typically on a weekend), during which a Danjiri Festival is celebrated. (This festival float is of the Takarazuka type.)

== Cultural Properties ==
* The main hall has been designated as a cultural property by Takarazuka City.

== Access ==
* A 8-minute walk from the {{ill|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|en|Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|WD=Q3832859|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} station {{ill|Yamamoto Station|ja|山本駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Station|1=Q23806826|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23806826}}.

== Surroundings ==
* Nearby is {{ill|Daihō-ji|ja|大宝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daihō-ji|1=Q11434344|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11434344}}, which—based on a syncretic arrangement—is adjacent; its wooden {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō|1=Acala}} and a treasure pagoda ({{ill|Hōkyōintō|en|Hōkyōintō|WD=Q3146365|lt=Hōkyōintō|1=Hōkyōintō}}) are designated cultural properties of Takarazuka City.

== References ==

{{Reflist}}
== See Also ==

* {{ill|Gion cult|en|Gion cult|WD=Q11588425|lt=Gion Faith|1=Gion Faith}}
